title: CSS Clear
content:
### CSS Clear **clear** controls whether an element can sit next to floated elements. --- ### Clear Values | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | `none` | Default - allows float on both sides | | `left` | Cleared below left floats | | `right` | Cleared below right floats | | `both` | Cleared below both left and right floats | | `inline-start` | Cleared below start floats | | `inline-end` | Cleared below end floats | --- ### Basic Example ```css .box { clear: both; /* Moves below any floated elements */ } ``` --- ### Clear: Left ```css .element { clear: left; /* Element goes below left-floated elements */ } ``` --- ### Clear: Right ```css .element { clear: right; /* Element goes below right-floated elements */ } ``` --- ### Clear: Both ```css .footer { clear: both; /* Always below all floated elements */ } ``` --- ### The Clearfix Hack To fix parent collapse from floated children: ```css .clearfix::after { content: ""; display: table; clear: both; } ``` Usage: ```html
Left
Right
``` --- ### Modern Alternative ```css .parent { display: flow-root; /* Creates block formatting context */ } ``` `display: flow-root` is the modern way to contain floats without clearfix. --- ### When to Use Clear | Scenario | Use | |----------|-----| | Footer after floated content | `clear: both` | | Next section after image float | `clear: both` | | Fix collapsed parent | `.clearfix::after` or `display: flow-root` | | Specific side clearance | `clear: left` or `clear: right` | > **Note:** With modern Flexbox/Grid layouts, float and clear are rarely needed except for text wrapping around images.
codeExample:
Float L
Float L
Float R
This div has clear:both - below all floats

quiz.question: Which clear value moves element below all floats?
